title: alpinia aquatica
intro: Alpinia aquatica, also known as the water ginger or water screwpine, is a type of plant belonging to the ginger family (Zingiberaceae) and is native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Alpinia aquatica include reducing fever, alleviating pain and inflammation, and treating digestive issues. Alpinia aquatica can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed for its medicinal properties.
